{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c79d511af02b4d179a660dbe","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\n3\tdescription: \"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: feedback\n7\t  originSessionId: 96b52212-e56d-4bfe-8793-3862c103275a\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. But that signal alone does NOT mean the work failed — the bead can be **complete-but-stuck-open**, typically because prior `br close` attempts silently failed (see [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]] and [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]). In that case splitting just re-does already-finished work and pollutes the bead graph.\n11\t\n12\t**Concrete case (bf-34lwt, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired asking to re-investigate a REJECT/token root cause. But the bead was already split into 3 closed children (bf-5ig3e/bf-29wyl/bf-2hdbg), had its deliverable posted on the parent (bf-4iewr) **three times** as comments, plus a committed write-up (`docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md`), and had been re-verified at HEAD twice. It was stuck open only because the templated `br close --body` kept failing. `br close bf-34lwt` (no flags) closed it instantly and broke the loop.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply — before honoring an Auto-Split dispatch, check whether the work is already done:**\n15\t1. `br comments list <parent-of-this-bead>` — is the deliverable already posted?\n16\t2. `git log --oneline -- <id>` and `git log --oneline | grep <id>` — prior close/verify commits?\n17\t3. Are its existing children already `closed` (`br show <each>`)?\n18\t4. Does the committed artifact exist?\n19\t\n20\tIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Do NOT split. Do NOT emit `SPLIT_COMPLETE`. If genuinely incomplete, then split per the dispatch.\n21\t\n22\t**Concrete case (bf-4mle6, 2026-07-07) — the OTHER branch (genuinely incomplete, so DO split, but scope to gaps):** Auto-split fired on a bead with 372 lines of *uncommitted* implementation in the working tree (`cmd/sim/main.go` provisioning real tokens via `/api/provision`) — but its acceptance criteria were NOT met: PROGRESS.md still described the now-obsolete old policy, nothing verified behavior under `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`, and the e2e diff was *mixed* with unrelated bf-4q5w (IO-6 hard-gate) test code. **How to apply when incomplete-but-already-implemented:** split into children scoped to the REMAINING GAPS (verify the claim → fix/test the harness → document+commit+close), and put \"the implementation ALREADY EXISTS in the working tree — do NOT re-implement\" at the top of the first child so no child re-does written code. Add a child to DISENTANGLE unrelated work mixed into the same diff (it belongs to a different bead — keep it out of this bead's commit/acceptance). Don't close incomplete/uncommitted work; the \"failures\" are usually the stuck-close symptom, not a sign the task is too big.\n23\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"041827b3-bbce-430d-9f88-ec344bb5d2ea","uuid":"5acc3a4b-136a-4135-8a57-7e9869fa5ff5","timestamp":"2026-07-07T14:18:25.007Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads.md","content":"---\nname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\ndescription: \"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 96b52212-e56d-4bfe-8793-3862c103275a\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. But that signal alone does NOT mean the work failed — the bead can be **complete-but-stuck-open**, typically because prior `br close` attempts silently failed (see [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]] and [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]). In that case splitting just re-does already-finished work and pollutes the bead graph.\n\n**Concrete case (bf-34lwt, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired asking to re-investigate a REJECT/token root cause. But the bead was already split into 3 closed children (bf-5ig3e/bf-29wyl/bf-2hdbg), had its deliverable posted on the parent (bf-4iewr) **three times** as comments, plus a committed write-up (`docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md`), and had been re-verified at HEAD twice. It was stuck open only because the templated `br close --body` kept failing. `br close bf-34lwt` (no flags) closed it instantly and broke the loop.\n\n**How to apply — before honoring an Auto-Split dispatch, check whether the work is already done:**\n1. `br comments list <parent-of-this-bead>` — is the deliverable already posted?\n2. `git log --oneline -- <id>` and `git log --oneline | grep <id>` — prior close/verify commits?\n3. Are its existing children already `closed` (`br show <each>`)?\n4. Does the committed artifact exist?\n\nIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Some sandbox limitation?\n\nWait — actually, maybe `pkill -f \"spaxel-mothership\"` matched and killed the parent bash because the command line contained \"spaxel-mothership\"! So `pkill -f \"spaxel-mothership\"` could match... no wait, pkill doesn't match itself usually. But it could match the PARENT shell whose command line includes \"spaxel-mothership\"!\n\nActually, pkill by default excludes itself, but it does NOT exclude its parent shell. If my bash command line (the whole script passed to bash) contains the string \"spaxel-mothership\", then `pkill -f \"spaxel-mothership\"` will match and kill my own shell! That's exactly what's happening. The shell gets SIGTERM (15) → exit 143, but here it's 144 = signal 16... close enough, the point is my own pkill is killing my own shell.\n\nThat's the bug. The pattern \"spaxel-mothership\" matches the bash process running my command (because the command text contains that string). pkill sends the default signal SIGTERM. I need to avoid pkill -f with a pattern that matches my own command line.\n\nLet me fix this: use a more specific pattern that won't match the shell, or kill by reading a pidfile, or just check the port and bind. Let me do that. But `ss -ltnp` needs privileges to show PID sometimes. Let me use a different approach.\n\nActually, let me reconsider. The earlier runs that \"failed\" were due to leftover mothership processes. But the harness checks `ss -ltn | grep :8199` as a precondition and refuses to run if the port is bound. So if there's a leftover, the harness will FAIL gracefully rather than produce bad data.
